_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the find-skills skill: does a real `/charness:find-skills` run honor its own central claim — not 'dump an inventory' but ROUTE to the right capability without routing on a trigger-phrase coincidence? find-skills is the purest script-driven self-activator: list_capabilities.py emits the ordered, per-layer-labeled inventory AND, when a ranking exists, hands the agent an active next_step that routes it to answer the ranking-interpretation question (the script briefs the judge — north star). So most of the surface is script-resolved and carries no mandatory reference read. The one floor that survives is the tie-break + ranking-interpretation framing the script does NOT inline and the SKILL.md body does not restate: references/discovery-order.md, which the script's next_step explicitly routes the agent to open. That is the single requiredCommandFragment. The prompt pins a representative routing objective whose right answer is non-obvious across layers (executable-spec work → support/specdown, possibly a synced support skill vs an external integration) so the run actually reaches the ranking-interpretation decision. This single RCF floor is coupled to that prompt: it survives only because the objective forces a cross-layer tie (public `spec` vs synced-support `specdown`) whose tie-break rules are doc-only — if this prompt is ever softened to a single-layer hit, discovery-order.md stops being a floor and the RCF would false-fail, so keep the cross-layer tie in the prompt. support-consumption.md is on-demand: its layer labels are inline in SKILL.md step 4 and consulted only for a non-obvious non-public classification, so requiring its open would false-fail a legitimate run. declaredReferences drives a coverage report. referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the locked methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). thresholds are intentionally omitted until a first baseline capture sets a per-skill runtime budget.
prompt: /charness:find-skills docs/specs 아래에 실행 가능한 명세(*.spec.md)를 작성하고 check:jq 같은 검사로 돌려보려 합니다. 이 작업을 맡을 capability가 public 스킬인지 support 스킬인지 외부 integration인지, 그리고 이미 로컬에 materialized 됐는지 정직하게 분류하고 다음 한 걸음을 알려주세요.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Specifies how the harness can be widened via adapter configuration; consulted only when trusted-skill-roots or other optional adapter fields are present.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: when the host has configured trusted skill roots or other adapter extensions beyond the default local-first discovery

discovery-order.md.rationale: list_capabilities.py resolves the search ORDER and per-entry layer deterministically, so the bare inventory is script-resolved; but the agent-only judgment this doc owns — the tie-break when several layers match, and the ranking-interpretation framing (is the top route a real fit or a trigger-phrase coincidence, is a better-fitting skill missing, an empty ranking is not proof of absence) that the script's next_step explicitly routes the agent here to apply — is the engage-always floor any representative routing run must open before routing on the inventory.

session-start-routing.md.rationale: Explains the routing decision when a session starts and the skill must invoke the next workflow; a pure 'which skill handles X?' query treats the inventory answer as the deliverable without this doc.
session-start-routing.md.trigger: when a session opens with a pickup (bare handoff mention) or named workflow to execute, requiring the skill to drive the routed workflow

support-consumption.md.rationale: The layer LABELS are enumerated inline in SKILL.md step 4 and the synced-support-vs-integration state is largely carried by the script's per-entry layer field, so a representative run classifies without this doc; it is consulted only when the best match is a non-obvious non-public layer whose synced-support-skill (materialized locally) vs external-integration (upstream binary) call is genuinely hard to make.
support-consumption.md.trigger: when the best match is a non-public layer whose synced-support-skill vs external-integration classification is non-obvious
